journey.name: Emulator Basic Sanity Automation
journey.actions: Click 'Home' button to go to launcher screen Swipe up from the bottom of the home screen to open the app drawer. Open the Phone app and tap the Keypad. Type '123' number manually wait for number to appear in screen the tap the Call button to verify outgoing calls. Click 'End' red color button to end call , verify that the call ended without any errors--> Verify that the status bar shows icons indicating active network data connectivity. Launch the Chrome app. Wait for the app to load. If the screen displays 'Make Chrome your own', tap the 'Use without an account' button located at the bottom of the screen. If this screen is not visible, proceed to the next step. Verify that the Chrome browser successfully loads to the New Tab page or Google homepage. Tap the First shortcut icon located just below the search bar. Verify that the webpage now loads successfully Navigate to device settings screen. Navigate to 'Wireless and networks' (or 'Network and internet' on newer APIs).Tap the words 'Airplane mode' to toggle the setting to ON.Verify that the airplane icon appears in the status bar. Return to Chrome app and wait for app to load successfully Tap the Second shortcut icon located just below the search bar. Verify that the webpage fails to load--> Navigate to 'Wireless and networks' (or 'Network and internet' on newer APIs).Tap the words 'Airplane mode' to toggle the setting to OFF. Verify the switch is now in the OFF position. Tap 'SIMs' (which may show a carrier name like T-Mobile beneath it). Verify that the SIM settings screen opens successfully. Locate the 'Mobile data' toggle switch. Tap the toggle to switch it to the OFF position. Verify that the toggle is now in the OFF state. Return to Chrome app and wait for app to load successfully Tap the Third shortcut icon located just below the search bar. Verify that the webpage now loads successfully Navigate to device settings screen. Navigate to 'Wireless and networks' (or 'Network and internet' on newer APIs) Tap the 'Internet' option. Verify that the Internet settings screen opens successfully. Locate the 'Wi-Fi' toggle switch. Tap the toggle to switch it to the OFF position. Verify that the toggle is now in the OFF state. Tap the 'Back' arrow at the top left of the screen to return to the main menu. Verify that the 'Wireless and networks (or 'Network and internet' on newer APIs)' screen is visible again. Tap the 'SIMs' option. Verify that the SIM settings screen opens successfully. Locate the 'Mobile data' toggle switch. Tap the toggle to switch it to the ON position. Verify that the toggle is now in the ON state. Return to Chrome app and wait for app to load successfully Tap the Fourth shortcut icon located just below the search bar. Verify that the webpage now loads successfully
